key_insight: Water in Rb2Ti2O5 self-organizes into 1D chains and hydrated (001) surface layers that enable fast, highly anisotropic proton transport.
engineering_relevance: These titanates could inform oriented proton-conducting materials for solid-state devices, where hydration state and crystal direction directly control conductivity.
method: Scanning electron microscopy to map hydration-dependent patterns and exfoliation
Density Functional Theory simulations to resolve water ordering and hydrogen bonding
Infrared spectroscopy plus conductivity measurements to link structure with proton transport
evidence_signal: The abstract reports consistent experimental and simulation evidence, including 1D water double chains at low hydration and superionic conductivity along b reaching 3 mS/cm at room temperature.
watch_item: Performance depends strongly on hydration level and crystal orientation, so maintaining the desired water ordering and stability may be challenging in practical devices.
paper_title: One-dimensional self-organization of water molecules in proton conducting Andersson-Wadsley titanates
